description: Push notification abuse — Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM), Apple Push Notification Service (APNs), web push (VAPID), and OneSignal. Common bugs: unauthenticated server-key send, push-token enumeration, silent-push exfil to mobile apps that handle silent payloads in background, cross-account token reuse on shared devices.
craft_guidance: 1. FINGERPRINT: FCM endpoint = `fcm.googleapis.com/fcm/send` or `fcm.googleapis.com/v1/projects/<id>/messages:send`. APNs = `api.push.apple.com`. OneSignal = `onesignal.com/api/v1/notifications`.
2. SERVER KEY LEAK: legacy FCM uses long-lived `Authorization: key=AAAA...` server key. Often hardcoded in mobile app or web bundle. Search JS bundles for `AAAA[A-Za-z0-9_-]{120,}`.
3. UNAUTH SEND: if backend exposes a /push/send endpoint that proxies to FCM without authenticating the caller, attacker sends arbitrary push to any registered token.
4. TOKEN ENUM: FCM tokens are 142+ chars but begin with predictable prefix patterns. Brute-forcing whole space is infeasible; harvesting via XSS/log leak/partner-API is common.
5. SILENT PUSH: `data` (no `notification`) does NOT show UI but wakes app. Mobile app may execute arbitrary handler logic. If handler reflects payload data into JS bridge, RCE-class on mobile.
6. CROSS-ACCOUNT TOKEN: same physical device, two user logins = same FCM token. Server that pushes by token (not by user-id mapping) sends victim's payload to attacker.
7. NOTIFICATION CONTENT INJECTION: notification.body / title rendered as text — but if app passes through Markdown/HTML, XSS-on-notification possible.
8. DEEP LINK INJECTION: notification click_action / data.url often opens an in-app webview with attacker URL — phishing on the trusted app surface.
9. PERSISTENCE: subscribe attacker to your notifications via shared FCM topic (`/topics/<name>`); if topic creation is unauthenticated, anyone can subscribe.
10. COLLABORATOR: silent push with `data: { exfil_url: 'COLLABORATOR' }` — if app handler fetches from `data.exfil_url`, OOB confirmation.

fcm_server_key_in_bundle.description: FCM legacy server key embedded in client (web/mobile) bundle. Server key has full project send privilege.

probes.description: Search JS / mobile bundle for FCM server key pattern (AAAA prefix, 152 chars). Hit = full project compromise.

fcm_unauth_send_proxy.description: Backend exposes `/api/push/send` or `/notifications/send` that proxies to FCM without authenticating the caller (or with caller-supplied target token).

probes.description: Send notification through backend proxy with attacker-supplied token. If 200 + the test push delivers, no caller auth.

silent_push_exfil.description: Data-only push wakes mobile app handler in background. If handler trusts payload (e.g. fetches `data.url`), attacker has RCE-class primitive.

probes.description: Silent push with attacker-controlled URL in data. If victim app makes a fetch to COLLABORATOR, payload-driven SSRF on mobile confirmed.

topic_subscribe_unauth.description: FCM topics let any token subscribe. If app uses topic for sensitive notifications without server-side validation of subscriber identity, attacker subscribes and receives all topic broadcasts.

probes.description: Subscribe attacker token to high-value topic. If 200 and subsequent topic broadcasts reach attacker, isolation broken.

notification_deep_link_injection.description: Push payloads carry click_action / data.url that an in-app webview opens. Server-side trust of caller-supplied URL = phishing on the app's trusted surface.

probes.description: Notification with attacker click_action. Victim taps -> in-app webview loads attacker URL with the user's session cookies.

cross_account_token_reuse.description: Same physical device + two user logins = same FCM token (Firebase doesn't bind tokens to user). Backend that maps user_id->token without re-checking on send may push victim's notifications to attacker (after attacker logged in on shared device).

probes.description: [Manual] Verify by registering token under attacker account, then trigger event for victim account on the same backend. If notification delivers to attacker, mapping is wrong.
